title: Hanoi Data Solution Summit 2024: When 80% of Software Goes to Waste
excerpt: 120 C-level executives gathered in Hanoi to confront a provocative truth — most enterprises invest heavily in MarTech but activate less than a fifth of its potential. Here's what we learned.
author: AKA Digital Team
body_intro: The 2024 Data Solution Summit drew over 120 senior marketing and technology leaders to Hanoi for a frank conversation about the state of MarTech adoption in Vietnam and Southeast Asia. The central finding: despite significant investment in platforms and tools, the majority of capabilities go untouched.
key_takeaways: 80% of enterprises invest in MarTech software but only use 20% of its features
The gap between deployment and activation is the single biggest drain on marketing ROI
First-party data strategies are now a board-level priority, not just a tech team concern
CDP adoption in Vietnam is accelerating — brands that move now will own the customer relationship

title: Martech Summit 2025: From Data Chaos to Performance Delivery
excerpt: AKA Digital's flagship annual event returned in 2025 with a laser focus on AI-powered automation, zero-party data collection, and the human strategies behind measurable campaign performance.
author: AKA Digital Team
body_intro: The 2025 Martech Summit marked a turning point in how Southeast Asian brands think about marketing performance. Held in Ho Chi Minh City, the event brought together over 400 marketing and technology leaders under the theme 'From Data Chaos to Performance Delivery' — capturing exactly where the industry finds itself: awash in data, hungry for results. Speakers from FE Credit, Vietjet Air, and regional brands across SEA shared the hard lessons of building marketing machines that actually move revenue. The central thread running through every session: the brands winning in 2025 are not those with the most data — they are those who have built systems to act on it fastest.
key_takeaways: AI-assisted personalisation is now table stakes; brands without it are losing ground to competitors who already have it
Zero-party data — information customers willingly share — is the new competitive moat that can't be bought or scraped
The winning brands in 2025 will be those who close the gap between data collection and real-time action
Marketing automation ROI compounds over time; the best time to start was last year — the second best time is now
Vietnam brands are leading SEA in first-party data adoption, driven by early CDP investments in 2022–2023

title: Martech Summit 2024: Personalisation at Scale — Lessons from Vietnam's Top Brands
excerpt: A look back at the 2024 Martech Summit — where hundreds of marketing leaders gathered to hear how Vietnam's most ambitious brands are executing personalisation across millions of customer touchpoints.
author: AKA Digital Team
body_intro: The 2024 Martech Summit brought together hundreds of digital marketing leaders from Vietnam's leading enterprises — financial services, aviation, e-commerce, and retail — for a day of candid conversation and applied learning.
key_takeaways: Personalisation at scale requires both the right technology and the right data governance
Customer journey mapping is the foundation of every successful automation programme
Vietnam brands are leapfrogging legacy systems — building modern stacks from scratch
Platform integration is as important as platform selection

title: CDP Deepdive: Should Your Brand Build a Customer Data Platform in 2024?
excerpt: In collaboration with mParticle, AKA Digital hosted a hands-on workshop for heads of technology and marketing across Southeast Asia — exploring whether a CDP is right for your brand, and how to build one that actually works.
author: AKA Digital Team
body_intro: Customer Data Platforms have gone from niche infrastructure to board-level conversation in the space of three years. But for many brands in Southeast Asia, the question isn't whether to invest in a CDP — it's how to make one deliver real value from day one. In this hands-on session delivered in partnership with mParticle, AKA Digital walked senior technology and marketing leaders through the architecture decisions, data governance frameworks, and activation strategies that separate successful CDP deployments from expensive shelf-ware.
key_takeaways: A CDP is only as good as the data flowing into it — identity resolution is step zero
Start with one high-value use case and expand; don't try to boil the ocean
Real-time data activation is the differentiator — batch processing is last decade
Third-party cookie deprecation makes first-party data infrastructure non-negotiable

title: Martech Innovation Day 2023: Vietnam's Definitive MarTech Conference
excerpt: Over 300 senior marketers and technology leaders gathered for Vietnam's premier MarTech conference — exploring real-world applications of AI, automation, and customer data platforms reshaping how brands grow.
author: AKA Digital Team
body_intro: Martech Innovation Day 2023 brought together Vietnam's most forward-thinking marketing and technology leaders in Ho Chi Minh City for a landmark industry event. Over 300 senior professionals from financial services, aviation, e-commerce, FMCG, and retail joined AKA Digital and its technology partners to explore the strategies, platforms, and mindset shifts redefining marketing performance across Southeast Asia. The day centred on three converging forces: the rise of AI-assisted marketing, the urgency of first-party data infrastructure, and the practical challenge of turning platform investment into measurable business outcomes. Keynotes covered the evolution of customer data platforms from technical projects to C-suite priorities, alongside hands-on demonstrations of marketing automation, CDP activation, and programmatic campaign optimisation. Panel discussions brought candid perspectives from brands who had navigated the journey from fragmented MarTech stacks to unified, ROI-generating marketing systems.
key_takeaways: Vietnam's MarTech maturity is accelerating faster than the rest of SEA — driven by young, digital-native consumer bases in banking, aviation, and e-commerce
CDP adoption was the defining theme: brands with unified customer data outperformed peers on every campaign metric
AI is already being used for segmentation, lookalike modelling, and content personalisation — not just as a future concept
The biggest barrier to marketing automation ROI is not technology — it's the process and team structure around it
First-party data programmes built in 2023 will become competitive moats by 2025

title: Mixpanel vs. GA4: Choosing the Right Analytics Stack for Growth
excerpt: When should you move beyond Google Analytics 4? AKA Digital hosted a frank roundtable with product, data, and marketing leaders — exploring when Mixpanel wins, when GA4 is enough, and what the choice tells you about your analytics maturity.
author: AKA Digital Team
body_intro: Google Analytics 4 is free, widely deployed, and deeply integrated with the Google advertising ecosystem. Mixpanel is purpose-built for behavioural analytics, product-led growth, and event-driven data — with a pricing model that reflects that depth. For most brands in Vietnam and Southeast Asia, the question is not which tool is better — it is which tool matches your analytics maturity, your team structure, and the business questions you are actually trying to answer. AKA Digital convened a roundtable of product managers, data leads, and marketing directors from financial services, e-commerce, and media businesses to work through the real-world decision. The conversation was direct, specific, and grounded in the actual data challenges facing SEA brands in 2023. The central finding: GA4 is the right starting point for acquisition-focused marketing teams. Mixpanel earns its place when you need to understand what users do inside your product, identify drop-off in activation or conversion funnels, and run A/B tests with genuine statistical rigour across user cohorts.
key_takeaways: GA4 is the right tool for brands focused on acquisition: paid search, SEO, social, and attribution — it integrates natively with Google Ads and BigQuery
Mixpanel wins when you need to understand user behaviour inside your product: funnels, retention cohorts, feature adoption, and event-level path analysis
The two tools are not mutually exclusive — many mature brands run both: GA4 at the marketing layer, Mixpanel at the product layer
Event taxonomy is the make-or-break factor: a well-designed event schema in either tool outperforms a poorly implemented version of the other
For Vietnam and SEA brands, the key question is team capability — do you have a data analyst who can build and maintain Mixpanel dashboards? If not, start with GA4
